title: Performance Domain Entry
use_for: phase, command, storage, scheduler, and diagnostics performance contracts
Unity-hosted runtime optimization with Unity kept at the host boundary
phase budget and overflow strategy design
do_not_use_for: feature ownership without a performance goal
task-specific bottleneck decisions without the owning task domain
UI architecture as a primary owner
plugin integration as a primary owner
assembly analysis as a primary owner

primary_when: request is primarily about measurable runtime behavior, hot-path architecture, contention, memory discipline, or scheduling strategy
request is about designing a system around predictable CPU cost, cache locality, allocation control, strict lifecycle ordering, or explicit work budgets
must_not_own_when: request requires task-specific decisions that depend on a more concrete feature or execution domain and performance is only shaping support
request is primarily about feature-domain behavior without performance ownership
request is about UI composition, plugin lifecycle, or assembly parsing with only incidental performance concerns

references.title: Performance Domain Foundations
references.interpretation: Use as the default operating frame for cost model, measurement discipline, and optimization boundaries.
